steps.title: Find the distant-local contrast
steps.content: The claim says Monroe's speaker criticizes activists who champion humanitarian causes elsewhere while overlooking local suffering. The right quotation must contrast pleading for people from far away with remembering people suffering nearby.

steps.title: Reject quotes without that contrast
steps.content: A praises the native land without criticizing selective activism. B addresses justice for the poor and lowly but not foreign versus local concern. C describes people of different sects and creeds joining together, not neglected local suffering. The answer is D.
